text: Meeting deadlines is an essential skill in both academic and professional settings. Effective time management begins with breaking large projects into smaller, manageable tasks and setting mini-deadlines. Prioritization helps you focus on the most important or urgent items first, rather than getting stuck on minor details. It is also important to manage the scope of your work—if the project grows beyond its original plan, you may need to negotiate what is realistically achievable. Finally, communication is key: if you foresee a delay, inform your team or supervisor early so adjustments can be made. By combining these strategies, you can reduce stress and deliver quality work on time.
